title: rubus × membranaceus
intro: Rubus × membranaceus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a hybrid of the Rubus idaeus (European raspberry) and Rubus fruticosus (blackberry), characterized by its edible and flavorful fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × membranaceus include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The fruit can be consumed fresh, dried, or used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and jams.
